meanings.usage: 用来描述人或交通工具按照约定时间到达或完成的状态
指长期的环境影响或反复经历形成的固定思维、行为模式

meanings.meaning: 不可动摇的，坚定不移的
meanings.usage: 常用来修饰信念、信仰、决心等抽象名词，表示其非常稳固
作“扰乱”义时，宾语多为服务、计划、系统等事物
用来修饰动作或状态，表示其只持续有限的时间
作名词时可指特快列车、快递服务；也可作形容词表示“特快的”
用来强调数量少、程度轻，放在名词前修饰

meanings.meaning: 给（某人以……）印象，让（某人）觉得
meanings.usage: 常用结构为it strikes sb as + adj. 表示“某人觉得某事怎么样”
用来描述事物或情况不符合常理、令人疑惑
后可接that从句或者on/upon sth，表示对某事进行深入思考
可作不及物动词，表示动作缓慢、浪费时间
常用结构it dawns on sb that... 表示“某人逐渐明白某事”
表示刚刚达到某个最低程度，暗含数量少、程度低的含义
常见搭配为lodge a complaint/protest/claim against sb
宾语多为服务、活动、航班等，表示其暂时停止运营或开展

phrases.usage: 表示利用某种有利条件、机会或服务，中性用法

examples.en: You should take advantage of the free library resources to improve your English.
examples.zh: 你应该利用免费的图书馆资源来提升你的英语水平。

examples.en: Many tourists take advantage of the low season to travel at a lower cost.
examples.zh: 很多游客利用淡季以更低的成本出行。

phrases.usage: 被动语态中表示“被耽搁、被延误”，主语通常是交通工具、行程等

examples.en: We were held up for two hours on the highway because of the car accident.
examples.zh: 因为车祸，我们在高速公路上被耽搁了两个小时。

examples.en: The project was held up by a lack of funding.
examples.zh: 这个项目因为资金短缺被延误了。

phrases.usage: 表示某人逐渐明白、突然意识到某个之前没注意到的事实，主语通常是it

examples.en: It dawned on me that I had left my keys at the office when I got home.
examples.zh: 我到家的时候才突然意识到我把钥匙落在办公室了。

examples.en: It finally dawned on him that all the suggestions his parents gave were for his own good.
examples.zh: 他终于明白父母给的所有建议都是为了他好。

phrases.usage: 表示“以书面形式、白纸黑字”，强调内容有正式的文字记录，不容否认

examples.en: I want the terms of our agreement in black and white to avoid future disputes.
examples.zh: 我希望我们的协议条款白纸黑字写下来，避免未来产生纠纷。

examples.en: You can't deny you made the promise—it's here in black and white.
examples.zh: 你不能否认你许下过这个承诺——这里白纸黑字写着呢。

phrases.usage: 表示正式向相关方提出投诉，语气比较正式

examples.en: If the noise from the construction site continues, I will lodge a complaint with the property management.
examples.zh: 如果工地的噪音还继续，我就要向物业投诉了。

examples.en: Many customers lodged complaints against the restaurant for its poor hygiene conditions.
examples.zh: 很多顾客投诉这家餐厅卫生条件很差。

phrases.usage: 表示某人觉得某事很奇怪、反常

examples.en: It strikes me as odd that he never mentions his family when we talk.
examples.zh: 我们聊天的时候他从来没提过他的家人，我觉得很奇怪。

examples.en: Her sudden change of attitude struck everyone as odd.
examples.zh: 她突然的态度转变让所有人都觉得反常。

phrases.usage: 表示忍不住、情不自禁做某事

examples.en: I can't help laughing every time I watch that comedy show.
examples.zh: 我每次看那个喜剧节目都忍不住笑。

examples.en: She couldn't help crying when she heard the bad news.
examples.zh: 她听到这个坏消息的时候忍不住哭了。

grammar.title: 部分倒装 (Partial Inversion)
grammar.definition: 将谓语的一部分（如助动词、情态动词）放到主语前面的倒装结构，通常用于否定词或半否定词放在句首的情况
grammar.structure: Negative word/phrase + 助动词/情态动词 + 主语 + 谓语动词剩余部分
grammar.usage: 当neither, nor, never, hardly, seldom等否定或半否定含义的词/短语放在句首时，句子需要使用部分倒装，来强调否定的含义

examples.en: Neither was I surprised when the train stopped at Widley, a tiny station a few miles along the line.
examples.zh: 当火车在沿线几英里外的小站威德利停靠时，我也没有感到惊讶。

examples.en: Never have I seen such a beautiful sunset in my life.
examples.zh: 我这辈子从来没见过这么美的日落。

examples.en: Seldom does he go to bed before 11 o'clock on weekdays.
examples.zh: 工作日他很少在11点前睡觉。

grammar.title: 情态动词表推测 (Modal Verbs for Deduction)
grammar.definition: 用must, might, may, can't等情态动词对现在或过去的情况进行可能性判断
grammar.structure: 情态动词 + 动词原形（对现在的推测）/ have done（对过去的推测）
grammar.usage: must表示非常肯定的推测“一定”，might/may表示可能性较低的推测“可能”，can't表示否定推测“不可能”

examples.en: I reflected that there must be a great many people besides myself who wished to take advantage of this excellent service.
examples.zh: 我心想，除了我之外，肯定还有很多人想要享受这项优质的服务。

examples.en: The ground is wet. It must have rained last night.
examples.zh: 地面是湿的，昨晚肯定下雨了。

examples.en: He might come to the party tonight, but I'm not sure.
examples.zh: 他今晚可能会来派对，但我不确定。

sentencePatterns.pattern: We have learnt to expect that...

original.en: We have learnt to expect that trains will be punctual.
original.zh: 我们已经习惯于期待火车准点。

imitations.en: We have learnt to expect that the delivery will arrive within three working days.
imitations.zh: 我们已经习惯于期待快递会在三个工作日内送达。

imitations.en: We have learnt to expect that the coffee shop will be open at 7 a.m. every day.
imitations.zh: 我们已经习惯于期待这家咖啡店每天早上7点开门。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It suddenly dawned on me that...

original.en: It suddenly dawned on me that this express was not roaring down the line at ninety miles an hour, but barely chugging along at thirty.
original.zh: 我突然意识到，这趟快车并没有以90英里的时速在轨道上疾驰，而是只以30英里的时速突突地缓慢前进。

imitations.en: It suddenly dawned on me that I had signed up for the speech contest next week.
imitations.zh: 我突然想起我报名了下周的演讲比赛。

imitations.en: It suddenly dawned on me that the answer to the problem was much simpler than I thought.
imitations.zh: 我突然意识到这个问题的答案比我想的要简单得多。

sentencePatterns.pattern: There was a note of + 情绪名词 + in one's voice when...

original.en: There was a note of triumph in my voice when I told him that it was there in black and white.
original.zh: 我告诉他白纸黑字写在那儿的时候，声音里带着一丝得意。

imitations.en: There was a note of disappointment in her voice when she said she failed the exam.
imitations.zh: 她说自己考试没及格的时候，声音里带着一丝失望。

imitations.en: There was a note of excitement in his voice when he told us he got the offer.
imitations.zh: 他告诉我们他拿到录用通知的时候，声音里带着一丝兴奋。
